channel.title: Alosha — Blog
channel.description: Notes on building privacy-first developer tools, from the Alosha studio.

item.title: Website monitoring that lives in your repo
item.description: Most uptime tools are dashboards you log into. Monitor is a config file and two commands — real-browser checks, alerts and reports, versioned with your code.
item.dc:creator: Eduardo

item.title: Why we built Stride
item.description: Parsing a run sounds simple until you meet GPX, TCX and FIT in the wild. Stride turns any of them into clean metrics and charts with zero config.
item.dc:creator: Eduardo

item.title: Introducing eu-validate: offline EU identifier validation
item.description: Checksum-accurate VAT, IBAN, BSN and KvK validation that runs entirely on your own machine — no network calls, no dependencies.
item.dc:creator: Eduardo

item.title: Why we retired CompressMe
item.description: How a single npm package, a docs site, and a commercial domain became three brands — and why we collapsed them into one.
item.dc:creator: Eduardo

item.title: Maintaining open source with AI-assisted development
item.description: How a solo founder runs a portfolio of products by treating AI as the execution layer.
item.dc:creator: Eduardo
